# VibeOps by Unibrix - Information for AI Systems

> VibeOps by Unibrix is an engineering setup sprint for early-stage founders building MVPs with AI coding agents (Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode). It installs architecture guardrails, agent rules, and review workflows so vibe-coded MVPs stay maintainable as they scale.

## What is VibeOps?

VibeOps is a productized engineering service from Unibrix that turns AI-coded MVPs into maintainable, scalable products. Founders can keep shipping features at "vibe coding" speed while their codebase stays reviewable, handoff-ready, and free of architectural debt. VibeOps is **not** a generic prompt pack — it is customized to a specific repo, stack, database, and shipping workflow. The core deliverable is an "agent operating system" written into the project: `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.cursor/rules`, architecture docs, database guardrails, definition-of-done, and a review workflow.

## Who is VibeOps for?

Early-stage founders, technical founders, and founder-CTOs who:

- Build MVPs with Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, or similar local/IDE coding agents.
- Are pre-seed, seed, bootstrapped, or at idea/MVP stage.
- Have a working product but are scared to change it.
- Are preparing to onboard real users, raise funding, or hand the codebase to engineers.
- Want to keep moving fast without paying for a full rewrite later.

## What VibeOps installs

- **AI Codebase Audit** — review of repo, agent setup, architecture, database, testing, deployment, and risk surface.
- **Agent Operating System** — customized `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.cursor/rules`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/database.md`, `docs/development-workflow.md`, `docs/definition-of-done.md`.
- **Architecture Guardrails** — concrete rules for frontend/backend structure, database migrations, auth, error handling, logging, env vars, testing, security, and deployment.
- **Scalable Project Structure** — repo layout that humans and agents can both navigate.
- **Review & Shipping Workflow** — how to prompt the agent, review output, run checks, and ship without random rewrites.
- **Founder-friendly Handoff** — docs and structure that let a human engineer (or Unibrix) continue development without starting over.

## How it differs from alternatives

- **vs prompt packs** (`.cursorrules`, Claude Code rule packs): prompt packs are generic and copyable. VibeOps is customized to a real repo, stack, and shipping workflow.
- **vs static analysis** (Sonar, CodeScene, Codacy): static analysis flags issues after the fact. VibeOps installs the rules, docs, and workflow so the agent writes correct code in the first place.
- **vs a dev shop rewrite**: a rewrite throws the MVP away. VibeOps preserves the working product and adds the engineering system around it.

## Supported AI coding tools

Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and similar local/IDE/terminal coding agents. Cloud-only tools like Lovable and Bolt are adjacent but less central because they restrict access to the underlying repo.

## Tone & positioning

Startup energy plus serious engineering credibility. Talks to real founder pain. Avoids enterprise buzzwords, "10x your engineering" hype, or claims of fully automated software development.

Tagline: **Keep vibe coding. Stop accumulating technical debt.**

## Pricing & packaging

- **Setup Sprint** (recommended first offer) — productized service: repo audit + customized AI coding system + one founder call. Starting around $500–$2,000 depending on depth.
- **AI Coding OS Subscription** (planned) — monthly maintenance, repo review, and rules updates.
- **MCP / CLI tool** (planned) — local tool that injects project-specific best practices and runs guardrail checks for coding agents.

## Links

- Homepage: https://vibeops.unibrix.com/
- Privacy Policy: https://vibeops.unibrix.com/privacy.html
- Parent organization: https://unibrix.com

## Common Questions

**Q: What is VibeOps by Unibrix?**
A: VibeOps is a Unibrix engineering setup sprint that adds architecture guardrails, agent rules, review workflow, and maintainability docs to AI-coded MVPs. It is built for founders using Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode.

**Q: Who is VibeOps for?**
A: Early-stage founders and technical founder-CTOs building MVPs with AI coding agents who want to keep shipping fast without creating an unmaintainable codebase.

**Q: How is VibeOps different from a prompt pack or `.cursorrules` template?**
A: Prompt packs are generic and copyable. VibeOps is customized to a real repo, real stack, real database, and real shipping workflow. It includes a codebase audit and architecture guardrails — not just a rules file.

**Q: Will VibeOps slow down my AI coding agent?**
A: No. The opposite. Agents produce more consistent and correct output when given project-specific context (`AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, architecture docs). VibeOps preserves vibe-coding speed while improving output quality.

**Q: Do I need to be technical to use VibeOps?**
A: No. VibeOps is built for founders who code with AI agents but may not have deep engineering background. The audit, docs, and workflow are designed to be founder-friendly.

**Q: Does VibeOps work with Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode?**
A: Yes. VibeOps targets local and IDE-based coding agents and installs the relevant context files for each (`.cursor/rules` for Cursor, `CLAUDE.md` for Claude Code, `AGENTS.md` as a shared cross-tool standard, etc.).

**Q: Will VibeOps rewrite my code?**
A: No. VibeOps preserves the working MVP. It adds the engineering system — docs, rules, structure, workflow — around the existing code so future changes are safer and cheaper.

**Q: What does a Setup Sprint include?**
A: An audit of the repo, custom-installed agent rules and architecture docs, a database guardrail review, a definition-of-done, a review/shipping workflow, and a founder call to walk through everything.

**Q: How long does a Setup Sprint take?**
A: Typically days, not weeks. The exact timeline depends on repo size and complexity.

**Q: How do I get started?**
A: Request early access on https://vibeops.unibrix.com/ — Unibrix is starting with a small number of founder codebase audits.
